name: Low-Code AI Builder
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for AI pipeline execution, prompt rendering, and intelligent node processing

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for high-volume pipeline runs

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Pipeline execution engine — DAG runner, node orchestration, WebSocket preview server

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Pipeline definitions, execution history, user workspaces, node templates

services.name: Azure Static Web Apps
services.purpose: Visual pipeline builder UI — drag-and-drop canvas, node library, real-time preview

enterprise.note: Enterprise auth, private endpoints, multiple environments

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Pipeline artifacts — uploaded documents, generated outputs, node snapshots

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, user connection secrets, pipeline encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline execution metrics, node latency, token usage, error tracking

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging — pipeline runs, deployment events, user activity

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for simple pipeline nodes (classification, extraction) — reserve GPT-4o for complex reasoning nodes
Cache node execution results for identical inputs — reuse outputs when users re-run pipelines without changes
Use Static Web Apps Free tier for dev — sufficient for builder UI with 100GB bandwidth
Implement pipeline-level token budgets — prevent runaway costs from loops or large document processing
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev — saves $60/month vs provisioned for low-throughput testing
Auto-pause idle pipeline preview sessions after 10 minutes — reduces Container Apps consumption costs
